title: Tensions rise between the United States and Iran with airstrikes and oil price increase
summary: The US conducts new airstrikes in Iran, while Trump assures there will be no war and oil prices rise due to the crisis.

title: Japanese 30-year bond yield reaches 2.9%, highest since 1996
summary: Long-term government debt exceeds 2.9% for the first time in 29 years, raising concerns about economic policy.

title: Two high school students missing after being swept away by a river in Shiga
summary: The teenagers were swept away by the current of the Yasu River while playing with friends, and the search continues by authorities.

title: Reform of the Imperial House Law advances in parliament towards approval
summary: The bill amending the imperial succession goes to a vote in the Lower House with support from several parties.

title: Bank of Japan maintains regional economic assessment unchanged in July
summary: The BoJ's Sakura Report maintains the outlook for the nine regions, with AI demand as a positive factor.

title: Samma fish reaches historic prices at first auction in Kushiro
summary: The first landing of samma sells for 440,000 yen per kilo, a record, and in stores it sells out at 80,000 yen per fish.

title: Nikkei index rises more than 900 points boosted by semiconductors
summary: The Tokyo stock market recovers ground with a rise of more than 900 points, following the bullish wave on Wall Street.

title: Calbee partially restores color to its packaging after ink shortage
summary: The snack company prints the front of packages in color again, though still keeps the back in black and white.

title: Suzuki recalls more than 500,000 vehicles in Japan
summary: The automaker recalls four models, including the Spacia, due to technical issues affecting half a million units.

title: Kenshiro Teraji shows his preparation for the super flyweight world title fight
summary: The Japanese boxer trains intensely ahead of his WBO title fight against Gonzales on July 20.

title: Prosecutors appeal 30-year sentence for group murder of a student in Ebetsu
summary: The Sapporo prosecutors consider the sentence imposed on Haoto Kawamoto insufficient for the death of a university student in a group attack.

title: Volcanic alert level raised on Kuchinoerabujima island due to increase in earthquakes
summary: The Japan Meteorological Agency warns of possible eruption with pyroclastic flows and projectiles, recommending caution.

title: Shohei Ohtani reaches 300 home runs in his MLB career
summary: The Japanese player hits his thirtieth home run of the season, reaching the 300 mark in the Major Leagues with an emotional gesture.

title: Roki Sasaki remains winless since May despite correcting his pitches
summary: The star pitcher for the Chiba Marines shows improvement but two errors cost him the victory.

title: Opposition parties introduce bill to expand aid to Minamata victims
summary: The initiative seeks to expand compensation criteria for those affected by the disease, although it has been rejected before.

title: Preparations for the Gion festival begin in Kyoto with the assembly of floats
summary: The traditional festival floats begin to be assembled, and the guardian deity figure of the Hokozan float is restored.

title: The Ryuo shogi tournament advances with key games among the challengers to Sota Fujii
summary: Players compete in the final phase of the tournament for the right to challenge for the title, with games of high strategic level.

title: Experiment on ISS reveals that lack of tactile stimulation accelerates aging
summary: Nematodes on the International Space Station show faster aging due to reduced physical contact.

title: Japan develops sodium-ion batteries for mass production without rare metals
summary: 14 organizations, led by Tokyo University of Science, collaborate on creating more sustainable next-generation batteries.

title: A Hong Kong fund sells its shares of Kakaku.com to LINE Yahoo
summary: The fund closes a tender offer agreement with the LINE Yahoo group for the acquisition of the price comparison platform.
